notes: NI Symphony Series Woodwind Ensemble — full orchestral woodwind section (flutes, oboes, clarinets, bassoons) recorded at Teldex Studio Berlin. CC 1 + CC 11 dual-dynamics approach applies here as for strings and brass — CC 1 cross-fades dynamic layers, CC 11 rides the level. Woodwinds are highly expressive; small CC 11 moves (5–10 values) create natural phrase shaping. Breath noise (QC 5) should be used sparingly — just enough to add life. Legato transitions require overlapping notes in the MIDI performance. Key switches trigger articulations (default C0–B0).

Expression.description: Expression — output level envelope. Small, continuous movements are key to realistic woodwind phrasing. The most natural-sounding CC for orchestral winds.

Modulation.description: Dynamics cross-fade — morphs between soft (pp) and full (ff) dynamic layers. Changes both volume and spectral character (reeds become brighter and more nasal at high velocities).

Breath.description: Breath controller — secondary dynamics or air pressure simulation

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds legato mode on some articulations

Quick Control 1.description: Ensemble Size — scales from solo-weight (thin, transparent) to full tutti section

Quick Control 2.description: Vibrato Depth — natural player vibrato intensity (0 = straight tone, useful for baroque or exposed solos)

Quick Control 3.description: Attack Speed — reed/flute speaking time; lower = soft and airy onset, higher = clean and articulate

Quick Control 5.description: Breath Noise — air and key noise level; adds intimacy and organic feel (use sparingly)

Quick Control 6.description: Release Time — note tail decay after key-up

Quick Control 7.description: Tonal Colour — reed character or embouchure simulation; higher = brighter, more open tone

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random detuning and timing variation between players

Stereo Width.description: Stereo image width of the woodwind section
